primaryObjectScope.expansionPolicy: Do not expand to other pages or Figma nodes until the SummaryDashboard workflow template is complete.

goalStatement: Use SummaryDashboard as the first complete example to run the reverse design-system workflow end to end, then reuse the workflow for future Figma nodes and design-system tasks.

reverseRuntimeTrack.purpose: Pursue high-fidelity Figma restoration and runtime validation inside local reverse surfaces.
reverseRuntimeTrack.promotionMeaning: High-fidelity restoration is evidence for review, not design-system promotion.

designSystemTrack.purpose: Extract transferable components, patterns, semantic tokens, concept models, state rules, and accessibility rules.
designSystemTrack.promotionPolicy: Promotion requires an explicit promotion cycle and remains blocked by default.

successCriteria: SummaryDashboard reverse surface has source-backed runtime validation for the scoped evidence.
Core candidate assets remain traceable through registry, contracts, reviews, runtime validation, and ADRs.
Storybook is used as runtime validation rather than a showcase.
Semantic tokens and CSS integration remain separated from promotion decisions.
The repo-file-driven workflow can advance one activeAction at a time and generate the next recommended action.
The completed workflow can be reused for later Figma nodes or design-system tasks.
notes: This goal is the durable repo-level summary of the user-confirmed workflow intent.
Future agents should read this file before executing activeAction.
The workflow target is both high-fidelity reverse runtime validation and conservative transferable design-system extraction.
